attr_description.label: Description
attr_description.value:
iglidur® GLW | Low-cost material for large volumes
With plain bearings made of the material iglidur® GLW, we can offer our customers an alternative to iglidur® G for large series applications. Featuring similar mechanical properties as iglidur® G, iglidur® GLW plain bearings are primarily recommended for static loads. These applications, where the properties of iglidur® G are unimportant to a degree , iglidur® GLW presents a cost-effective alternative.

Typical applications

Automotive
Machine tools
Robotics

Find more applications here:
Industry solutions

 
When to use it?
  • When an economical universal bearing for high volumes is required
  • For high, primarily static loads
  • For low to medium speeds
When not to use it?
  • When mechanical reworking of the wall surface is required
    iglidur® M250, sleeve bearing, mm
  • For primarily dynamic loads
    iglidur® G, sleeve bearing, mm
  • When extreme wear resistance is required
    iglidur® W300, sleeve bearing, mm
  • For use under water
    iglidur® H2, sleeve bearing, mm
Use our filter options to find the suitable product

Tolerances and measuring system
The installation dimensions and tolerances of the iglidur® plain bearings depend on the material and wall thickness. For each material, the moisture absorption and the thermal expansion are decisive factors. Plain bearings with low moisture absorption can be installed with a smaller bearing clearance. The rule for wall thickness: the thicker the wall of the bearings, the larger the clearances must be.
Testing methods

Assembly
iglidur® plain bearings are press-fit bearings. The inner diameter is only adjusted to the appropriate tolerance after pressing into the housing hole. The press-fit excess dimension can be up to 2% of the inner diameter. This ensures the secure press-fitting of the bearings. Axial or radial movements in the housing are thus reliably avoided.
iglidur® - assembling, gluing, machining

ABOUT-THERMAL-PROPERTIES.TITLE: Information on thermal properties
ABOUT-THERMAL-PROPERTIES.MAX_TEMPERATURE_LONG: The maximum continuous application temperature is the temperature the material can endure for a longer period of time without the properties changing considerably.
ABOUT-THERMAL-PROPERTIES.MAX_TEMPERATURE_SHORT: The maximum, short-term application temperature is the temperature above which the material becomes so soft, that it can only withstand small external loads. In this context, "short-term" is defined as a period of a few minutes.
ABOUT-THERMAL-PROPERTIES.MIN_TEMPERATURE: The minimum application temperature is the temperature below which the material is so rigid and hard that it becomes too brittle for standard applications.

ABOUT-CHEMICAL-PROPERTIES.TITLE: Information on chemical resistance
ABOUT-CHEMICAL-PROPERTIES.INFO_CONTENT: Only one individual check of each chemical is performed. Mixtures are not taken into account. All information is based on room temperature.
ABOUT-CHEMICAL-PROPERTIES.RESISTANT_TEXT:
• no significant changes in mechanical properties
  • no change in colour or surface
  • < 3 % weight increase or < 0.5 % weight loss possible
  • < 15 % change in compression force at break possible
  • < 2 change in Shore D hardness possible
According to available experience, no damage is caused by the medium.
ABOUT-CHEMICAL-PROPERTIES.PARTLY_RESISTANT_TEXT:
• noticeable changes in material weight and properties
  • slight change in colour and surface possible
  • 3 % to 8 % weight increase or 0.5 % to 3 % weight loss possible
  • 15 % to 25 % change in compression force at break possible
  • 2 to 5 change in Shore D hardness possible
We therefore recommend practical testing under the given conditions.

free-form-hub/materialsDescription.A160:
iglidur® A160 | FDA and EU regulation-compliant for chemicals and food

iglidur® A160 is characterised by extreme media resistance at low costs. Tribologically optimised, the material can be used in temperatures up to +90°C and also has the conformities required in the food processing sector. The property profile is rounded off by the "optical detectability" often desired in the industry, i.e. the blue colour.

Typical application areas

  • Beverage industry
  • Food industry
  • Medical technology

When do I use it?

  • When a material with the highest media resistance in the normal temperature range is required
  • When a very cost-effective material with high media resistance is required
  • When a material compliant with 10/2011 EC is required

When do I not use it?

  • If you need a universal material for the food industry
  • If you are looking for a highly media-resistant material for use at temperatures above +90°C
  • If you need a low-cost material with high wear resistance in dry operation
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.A180:
iglidur® A180 | The FDA-compliant all-rounder

The material iglidur® A180 is suitable for use in direct contact with food. It is therefore the ideal solution for bearing points on machines for the food and packaging industry, medical device construction, small household appliances, etc. The iglidur® A180 is also characterised by minimal moisture absorption wherever wet cleaning or process-related contact with moist media is the order of the day.

Typical application areas

  • Beverage industry
  • Food industry
  • Medical technology

When do I use it?

  • When my bearings come into direct contact with food
  • When FDA conformity is required When quiet operation is important
  • When low moisture absorption is required

When do I not use it?

  • When only the highest wear resistance is required
  • When temperatures permanently exceed +80°C
  • When a low-cost universal bearing is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.A181:
iglidur® A181 | The universal material for food contact

The iglidur® A181 material offers both FDA and 10/2011 EC conformity. The blue colour also facilitates the "optical detectability" frequently desired in the food sector. Directly comparable with iglidur® A180 in terms of mechanical specification, temperature and media resistance, iglidur® A181 is even better in terms of wear resistance in most constellations.

Typical application areas

  • Beverage industry
  • Food industry
  • Medical technology

When do I use it?

  • When FDA conformity is required
  • When a material conforming to 10/2011 EC is required
  • When a universal material for direct contact with food is required

When do I not use it?

  • If FDA and 10/2011 EC conformity are not required
  • If temperatures permanently higher than +90°C occur
  • If a low-cost universal bearing is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.A200:
iglidur® A200 | FDA-compliant for low speeds

The material iglidur® A200 is suitable for use in direct contact with food. It is therefore the ideal solution for bearing points on machines for the food industry, medical device construction, small household appliances, etc. Since the admixture of solid lubricants had to be dispensed with in favour of food compatibility, the thermoplastic alloy of iglidur® A200 is particularly adapted to abrasion resistance. Furthermore, iglidur® A200 is characterised by its ability to embed dirt and its smooth running behaviour.

Typical application areas

  • Food industry

When do I use it?

  • Suitable for direct contact with food
  • When a quiet operation is required
  • When dirt needs to be embedded When FDA conformity is required

When do I not use it?

  • If only the highest wear resistance is required
  • If temperatures permanently exceed +80°C
  • If a low-cost universal bearing is required
  • If it is to be used in a damp environment
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.A230: Specially developed for the food sector, iglidur® A230 is particularly suitable for machines and systems with food contact thanks to its optically easily detectable colouring, low moisture absorption and conformity with FDA and EU10/2011 guidelines. ​
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.A290: iglidur® A290 plain bearings were developed for good abrasion resistance and quiet operation. Compared to bearings made of the material iglidur® A200, the tribological and mechanical properties were significantly improved.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.A350-PF:
• Food compliant according to FDA and EU 10/2011

• Temperature resistant up to + 180°C

• Suitable for autoclave

Food-safe high-temperature filament, FDA-compliant

iglidur A350 is an abrasion-resistant high-temperature filament developed for the food and packaging industry. As such, the raw material is compliant for food contact in accordance with EU Regulation 10/2011 and the FDA, and comes in the industry-standard blue colour. It is also temperature-resistant up to 180°C, fire-retardant in accordance with UL94-V0 and can be sterilised with hot steam.

Tip: iglidur A350 also fulfils the fire safety requirements of the US Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for aircraft interiors

When do I use it?

• For applications in the food industry with high application temperatures of up to 180°C

When should I not use it?

• When high wear resistance at high temperatures is required

• If, in addition to high application temperatures, high strengths are also required or complex parts are to be manufactured (→ iglidur RW370)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.A500:
iglidur® A500 | The endurance runner at higher temperatures in the food sector

iglidur® A500 is approved for direct contact with food. It has an exceptionally good chemical resistance and is thus suitable for the most difficult applications in the environment of machines for the food industry. Although iglidur® A500 is a very soft material, it also has excellent compressive strength, even at high temperatures.

Typical application areas

  • Beverage industry
  • Food industry
  • Medical technology

When do I use it?

  • When FDA conformity is required
  • When very high chemical resistance is required
  • When good abrasion resistance is required
  • For temperature resistance from -100°C to +250°C

When should I not use it?

  • When the highest wear resistance is required
  • When high temperature and chemical resistance is not required
  • When a low-cost universal bearing is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.AB:
iglidur® AB | The first antibacterial iglidur® material

Components made of iglidur® AB help to reduce the germ load in bearing points that are difficult to access. iglidur® AB was specially developed for applications in areas with high hygiene requirements. Such applications often involve manually operated swivels (doors, medical furniture, etc.). The material reduces the germ load in the gap, but - like all "antibacterial" materials - is no substitute for appropriate hygiene measures.

Typical application areas

  • Medical technology
  • Laboratory technology
  • Ventilation systems
  • Sanitary furniture and equipment
  • Bearing points on patient and care furniture

When do I use it?

  • If the material has to fulfil high hygiene requirements
  • If you are looking for a universal material for manually operated applications
  • If you are looking for a material for low to medium loads

When do I not use it?

  • If a very wear-resistant material is required for continuous dry operation
  • If a material compliant with EU Regulation 10/2011 EC and/or in accordance with FDA requirements for repeated food contact is required
  • If a material with the highest universal media resistance is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.AX500: The special feature of iglidur AX500 is that it is a conductive material. In addition, AX500 has an even lower coefficient of wear than its predecessor. The material was specially developed for the food industry and can therefore be used in contact with food products.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.B:
iglidur® B | High flexibility and quiet
The compressive strength of iglidur® B is low, but this is an important specification of this material. It is mainly used where vibration dampening and acoustic decoupling are important.


When do I use it?

  • When maximum vibration dampening is required
  • When sealing functions are to be integrated
  • When high edge loads occur

When do I not use it?

  • In applications with high humidity
  • When a low-cost plain bearing is required
  • When maximum wear resistance is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.C:
iglidur® C | PTFE and silicone-free, good abrasion resistance
iglidur® C was specially developed for applications in which the use of PTFE and silicone is not possible. Such applications occur in electrical engineering, the tobacco and luxury food industry as well as in some painting processes. Keywords such as paint compatibility and freedom from silicone make it worthwhile to take a closer look at this material. 


When do I use it?

  • When good abrasion resistance is required
  • When PTFE or silicone are not approved for the application
  • For low-speed applications
  • When the bearing should be insensitive to dirt
  • When the bearing should be self-lubricating and suitable for dry operation

When do I not use it?

  • When maximum wear resistance is required
  • When low coefficients of friction is required
  • When a low-cost alternative is required
  • When low moisture absorption is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.C500:
iglidur® C500 | High media and temperature resistance, up to +250°C, wear-resistant

iglidur® C500 can be used in applications up to +250°C and is extremely media-resistant (e.g. in cleaning processes with hydrogen peroxide), wear-resistant and has a low coefficient of friction. Also suitable for various customised products.

Typical application areas

  • Automotive
  • Glass industry
  • Food industry
  • Mechanical engineering
  • Heavy machinery / Plant engineering

When do I use it?

  • When an extremely media-resistant material with high flexibility is required
  • When a very wear-resistant and media-resistant material is required

When do I not use it?

  • When an FDA-compliant high-temperature material is required
  • When a media-resistant and high-temperature-resistant material with a wide range of dimensions is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.D:
iglidur® D | Low-cost material with silicone

During the development of iglidur® D as a material, a very low price was at the top of the requirement profile. In addition, low coefficients of friction were to be achieved in dry operation, especially at high speeds. The silicone-containing material also achieves outstandingly low coefficients of friction in dry operation and runs largely stick-slip-free.

Typical application areas

  • Motorbike/scooter
  • Furniture/industrial design
  • Sport, fitness & rehab

When do I use it?

  • When a low coefficient of friction is required
  • At high speeds
  • At low loads
  • When a very inexpensive material is required

When should I not use it?

  • When high pressure loads occur
  • When the parts need to be silicone-free
  • When temperatures are permanently higher than +90°C
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.E: iglidur E is particularly characterised by its long service life in combination with shafts made of aluminium. Especially in linear and pivoting motion profiles, iglidur E offers particularly good wear properties. ​
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.E7: For the first time, the material E7, well-known from drylin® linear technology, is also available as a plain bearing with a wide range of dimensions. Bushings made of the new material offer excellent coefficient of friction and wear with low to medium loads.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.ECO-A180: Wear-resistant endurance material with very low coefficients of friction, vibration-damping and FDA-compliant
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.ECOH: The ECO group includes the iglidur® materials, which are made from at least 97% regranulate. iglidur® H is a fibre-reinforced thermoplastic material especially developed for applications in high atmospheric humidity or under water. Plain bearings made from iglidur® H can be used completely free of lubrication; in wet applications, the surrounding media acts as additional lubricant. Suitable for temperatures up to +200°C. Very low coefficient of friction when used with hardened shafts.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.ECOP: This group brings together the iglidur® materials that are made from at least 97% regranulate. Due to their thermal stability and low total moisture absorption, the iglidur® P plain bearings with flange are among the most dimensionally stable all-round bearings for changing ambient conditions. iglidur® P plain bearings are recommended for pivoting and rotational movements at medium loads.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.ECO-A500: Wear-resistant high-temperature material for the food sector (FDA and EU 10/2011 compliant), optically detectable
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.F:
iglidur® F | Electrically conductive, very pressure-resistant

Highest stiffness and hardness, and particularly conductive. When it comes to the electrical conductivity of materials, especially in applications where electrostatic charging must not occur, iglidur® F is the right choice. In addition, iglidur® F is very pressure-resistant. At room temperature, it can be statically loaded with up to 105MPa.

Typical application areas

  • Automotive
  • Textile industry

When do I use it?

  • If the material needs to be electrically conductive
  • For high static pressure loads

When do I not use it?

  • When mechanical rework is required
  • When maximum wear resistance is required
  • When a very low coefficient of friction is required in dry operation
  • For use under water
  • When a universal material is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.F2:
iglidur® F2 | ESD-compatible, wear-resistant and dissipative

iglidur® F2 helps to avoid electrostatic charging. Good media and temperature resistance, usability even in humid environments due to low moisture absorption as well as good universal wear values result in a wide range of applications.

Typical application areas

  • Mechanical engineering, robot industry

When do I use it?

  • If the material needs to be electrically conductive
  • If a universal material is required for a wide range of applications

When do I not use it?

  • When a universal material without static dissipative properties is required
  • For underwater use
  • When maximum wear resistance is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.G:
iglidur® G | The all-rounder

The widest range of different requirements is covered by iglidur® G. This is why the material is rightly described as universal. Applications with medium to high loads, medium surface speeds and medium temperatures are recommended. iglidur® G is the decathlete among the iglidur® materials. Extraordinarily good in all technical disciplines and above all the classic all-rounder in the sum of the general, mechanical, thermal and tribological specifications.

Typical application areas

  • Agricultural engineering
  • Automotive
  • Construction machinery
  • Mechanical engineering
  • Sport, fitness & rehab

When do I use it?

  • If I need an economical all-round material
  • For low to medium surface speeds
  • If the material needs to be suitable for different shafts
  • For pivoting and rotation applications

When do I not use it?

  • If mechanical rework is required
  • If the very highest wear resistance is required
  • If universal chemical resistance is required
  • If temperatures are continuously higher than +130°C
  • For use under water
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.G1:
iglidur® G1 | The new lubrication-free standard

With iglidur® G1, we offer a wear-resistant and versatile material for a wide range of applications. Applications with medium to high loads at medium speeds are recommended. Even at elevated temperatures up to 180°C, iglidur® G1 is dimensionally stable and wear-resistant and offers very low moisture absorption. This makes iglidur® G1 suitable for a large number of different applications and thus represents an all-rounder with very good running performance.

Typical application areas

  • Automotive
  • Mechanical engineering
  • Furniture / industrial design
  • Sport, fitness & rehab

When do I use it?

  • If I need an economical all-round material
  • For low to medium surface speeds
  • If the material needs to be suitable for different shafts
  • For pivoting and rotation applications

When do I not use it?

  • If mechanical rework is required
  • If the very highest wear resistance is required
  • If universal chemical resistance is required
  • If temperatures are continuously higher than +180°C
  • For use under water
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.GLW:
iglidur® GLW | Low-cost material for high volume

With the iglidur® GLW material, we can offer our customers an alternative to iglidur® G for high-volume applications. With similar characteristic values as iglidur® G, iglidur® GLW is particularly recommended for primarily static loads. For these applications, where the dynamic specifications of iglidur® G can be largely dispensed with, they represent a very cost-effective alternative.

Typical application areas

  • Automotive
  • Mechanical engineering
  • Robotics industry

When do I use it?

  • When I need an economical all-rounder material for high volumes
  • For high, primarily static loads
  • At low to medium speeds

When do I not use it?

  • When mechanical rework of the wall surface is required
  • For mainly dynamic loads
  • When maximum wear resistance is required
  • For use under water
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.GV0:
iglidur® G V0 | V0 classification according to UL94, universal

iglidur® GV0 is the first iglidur® material with V0 rating according to UL94 for universal use in the normal temperature range and is thus predestined for applications with high fire protection requirements (vehicle interior, aviation, building technology, etc.). All other iglidur® materials with V0 rating belong to the high temperature segment. The general mechanical and thermal specifications are largely comparable with the all-rounder iglidur® G.

Typical application areas

  • Passenger seats
  • Elevators
  • Escalators
  • Switch cabinets
  • Hinges

When do I use it?

  • When a UL94 V0-rated material is required for normal environmental conditions
  • When an economical UL94V0-rated material is required

When do I not use it?

  • When a UL94 V0 classified material is required for high operating temperatures
  • When a standard material is required without compliance with special fire standards
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.H:
iglidur® H | High temperature and chemical resistance

iglidur® H is a fibre-reinforced thermoplastic material that was specially developed for applications in high humidity or under water. iglidur® H can be used completely lubrication-free; when used in wet areas, the surrounding medium serves as an additional lubricant. Suitable for temperatures up to +200°C. Very low coefficient of friction in conjunction with hardened shafts.

Typical application areas

  • Beverage industry
  • Medical technology
  • Offshore
  • Shipbuilding

When do I use it?

  • For use under water
  • When high temperature resistance is required 
  • For high mechanical loads
  • For use in contact with chemicals

When should I not use it?

  • When the highest wear resistance under water is required
  • When the best universal chemical resistance is required
  • For the highest compressive strength at higher temperatures
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.H1:
iglidur® H1 | Endurance runner with high media resistance

iglidur® H1 is the first choice when long service life is required under extreme environmental conditions. Highest wear resistance is paired with outstanding temperature and media resistance - not only in packaging and food technology or automotive engineering.

Typical application areas

  • Vending machinery
  • Beverage industry
  • Textile industry
  • Packaging industry

When to use it?

  • When the highest service life under the influence of temperature and moisture is required
  • When low coefficient of friction and high temperature resistance are required
  • When aggressive cleaning is regularly carried out (splashing, steam blasting)
  • When the material is used in the engine compartment

When not to use it?

  • When high surface pressures occur
  • When only the best universal chemical resistance is required
  • When a cost-effective high-temperature material is required for which the best wear resistance is not important
  • When an FDA-compliant material with high temperature resistance is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.H2:
iglidur® H2 | Low cost, for high temperatures
When using iglidur® H2, economic aspects take centre stage. For the first time it is possible to offer a high-performance material for high volume with these technical advantages at such a favourable price. Temperatures up to +200°C, permissible surface pressure up to 110N/mm2 and very good chemical resistance. iglidur® H2 is self-lubricating and suitable for all movements.

Typical application areas

  • Automotive

When do I use it?

  • For use under water
  • When a low-cost material for high temperatures is required
  • For applications with fuels, oils, etc.
  • When chemical resistance is required

When not to use it?

  • When maximum wear resistance is required
  • When vibration dampening is required
  • When neither elevated temperatures nor media contact occur
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.H3:
<span style="colour: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">iglidur H3 | Tough, wear-resistant, durable
<span style="colour: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">With iglidur® H3, there is a new material in the iglidur® H family. H3 sets new standards with its high durability. This material is tougher than comparable materials and achieves a higher service life than iglidur H1. iglidur H3 was developed primarily for use in pumps and demanding media and impresses with its low moisture absorption.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.H370:
iglidur® H370 | Longest service life under water and high media resistance

iglidur® H370 is the right solution for underwater applications. The material absorbs very high loads, resists chemicals and can be used at temperatures up to +200°C.

Typical application areas

  • Fluid technology
  • Offshore
  • Shipbuilding
  • Heavy machinery / plant engineering
  • Packaging industry

When do I use it?

  • For use under water
  • When high temperature resistance is required
  • When high mechanical strength and wear resistance are required
  • When good chemical resistance is required

When do I not use it?

  • When mechanical rework of the material is required
  • When high wear resistance under temperatures is required
  • For use under heavy dirt
  • When an inexpensive high volume solution is needed
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.H4:
iglidur® H4 | The automotive standard for temperatures up to +200°C

iglidur® H4 stands for high load-bearing capacity, good abrasion resistance and good temperature resistance when economic aspects are also in the foreground. Temperatures up to +200°C, permissible surface pressure up to 65MPa, very good chemical resistance are just some of the essential attributes. Solid lubricants reduce the coefficient of friction and support the wear resistance, which has been significantly improved compared to the similarly very cost-effective iglidur® H2.

Typical application areas

  • Vending machinery
  • Automotive
  • Packaging industry

When do I use it?

  • For use with fuels, oils, etc.
  • When high wear resistance is required
  • For low coefficients of friction For high temperature resistance from -40°C to +200°C
    • For high chemical resistance

When should I not use it?

  • For underwater use
  • If you are looking for a low-cost universal material
  • If you are looking for a temperature-resistant and media-resistant material for predominantly static applications
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.H5: iglidur H5 was especially developed for the application in corrosive environments, in which high edge pressures and impact loads occur.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.HSD350:
iglidur® HSD350 | Media-resistant and hygienic at low cost

The new material enables hygienic continuous use including regular sterilisation with an excellent price-performance ratio.

Typical application areas

  • Fluid technology, medical technology

When do I use it?

  • If the bearing point is regularly sterilised with hot steam
  • If a low-cost material is required at the same time
  • If good chemical resistance is required
  • Low moisture absorption

When should I not use it?

  • When high pressure loads occur
  • When short-term temperatures greater than +120°C occur
  • When a cost-effective material for occasional movement is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.I10-PL:
• High chemical resistance

• Food compliant according to FDA

• Low moisture absorption

Chemical-resistant laser sintering material: iglidur i10

With iglidur i10, a chemical-resistant material has been developed that is characterised by high ductility and low moisture absorption. An ideal material for weight-saving functional components with elastic specifications, especially for applications in the electroplating industry. Due to its food conformity, iglidur i10 is also interesting for applications in the packaging and food industry.

When do I use it?

• For high demands on chemical resistance

• If the application requires a food-compliant material

• When elastic component properties are required

When do I not use it?

• For applications under water or with high humidity (→ iglidur i8-ESD)

• If components with ESD specification are required (→ iglidur i8-ESD or iglidur i9-ESD)

• If components with high mechanical strength are required, in particular gear applications (→ iglidur i3)

• When the long-term application temperature is higher than 60°C (→ iglidur i3)

• For all types of applications where low friction and high wear resistance are important
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.I150-PF:
• Food compliant according to EU 10/2011
• High abrasion resistance at low surface speeds

Abrasion-resistant filament - suitable for food contact

iglidur i150 is characterised by high wear resistance, as the material is enriched with solid lubricants. Thanks to its high wear resistance and good mechanical specifications (strength, toughness, layer adhesion), it is an all-round material that is suitable for most applications. The only restrictions are the maximum permanent application temperature of 65°C and for highly dynamic applications.

It is also suitable for food contact due to its conformity in accordance with EU 10/2011 and has proven itself in applications in the food and packaging industries.

When should I use it?
• For applications with low or medium surface speeds (safe up to 0.2m/s, test above this)
• If the application requires a food-compliant material

When should I not use it?
- If the long-term application temperature is higher than 65°C (→ iglidur i180 or iglidur J260 or iglidur A350 for food contact)
• For highly dynamic applications (→ iglidur i180)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.I151-PF:
• Food compliant according to FDA and EU 10/2011

• Blue colour for optical detectability

Abrasion-resistant filament for printing food-compliant components in blue colour

The tribofilament iglidur i151 is suitable for components with high wear resistance, as the material is enriched with solid lubricants. It was specially developed for use in the food and packaging industry. The components are compliant with FDA and EU Regulation 10 / 2011 and the blue colour ensures the required optical detectability. iglidur i151 is also an all-rounder material, as its good mechanical specifications (strength, toughness, layer adhesion) make it suitable for most applications. iglidur i151 has comparable mechanical specifications to iglidur i150, by far the most commonly used igus filament. The only restrictions are the maximum permanent application temperature of 65°C and for highly dynamic applications.

When do I use it?

• If the application requires a food-compliant material according to FDA or EU 10 / 2011

• When a visually detectable material is required

• For applications with low or medium surface speeds (safe up to 0.2m/s, test above 0.2m/s)

When should I not use it?

• If the application temperature is permanently higher than 65°C (→ iglidur i180 or iglidur J260 or iglidur A350 for food contact)

• For highly dynamic applications (→ iglidur i180)

• If food conformity according to FDA and the blue colour are not necessary (→ iglidur i150)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.I180:
• Versatile use: good combination of high wear resistance, strength and temperature resistance　

Self-lubricating filament iglidur i180

Components made from iglidur i180 can be used universally for all types of wear-resistant parts, such as plain bearings, gears and drive nuts, as this material has also been specially developed for moving applications. The higher wear resistance and higher temperature resistance (100°C long-term) extend the range of applications, especially for more dynamic applications with higher surface speeds.

When do I use it?
• When an increased long-term application temperature of up to 100°C is required.
• For highly dynamic plain bearing applications

When should I not use it?
• If the long-term application temperature is higher than 100°C or an even higher wear resistance is required (→ iglidur J260)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.I180_BL:
• Versatile: good combination of high wear resistance, strength and temperature resistance　

• Colour: Black

Self-lubricating filament iglidur i180-BL, black

Components made from iglidur i180-BL can be used universally for all types of wear-resistant parts, such as plain bearings, gears and drive nuts, as this material has also been specially developed for moving applications. The higher wear resistance and higher temperature resistance (100°C long-term) extend the range of applications, especially for more dynamic applications with higher surface speeds. 

When do I use it?

• When an increased long-term application temperature of up to 100°C is required.

• For highly dynamic plain bearing applications

• When a black material is required

When do I not use it?

• If the long-term application temperature is higher than 100°C or an even higher wear resistance is required (→ iglidur J260)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.I190:
• Flexural strength up to 80MPa, best iglidur filament

• Excellent service life - abrasion resistance up to 50 times better than ABS

Bend-resistant and wear-resistant filament - iglidur i190

iglidur i190 is characterised by high wear resistance, as the material is enriched with solid lubricants. Its high wear resistance and excellent mechanical specifications (strength, toughness, layer adhesion) make it an all-round material that is suitable for most applications. Of all igus tribofilaments, only iglidur RW370 has a higher strength than iglidur i190, making iglidur i190 the tribofilament with the highest strength. It can also withstand a long-term application temperature of up to 90°C (after tempering the component). 

When do I use it?

• For all types of applications where low friction and high wear resistance are important

• When higher strengths are required.

When do I not use it?

• If the long-term application temperature is higher than 90°C (→ iglidur J260 or iglidur RW370)

• For applications under water or with high humidity (→ iglidur i180)

• If the application requires a food-compliant material (→ iglidur i150 or iglidur A350)

• If an even easier to process tribofilament is required (→ iglidur i150)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.I230-PL:
• High application temperature up to 110°C

• Highest flexural strength of 94MPa at 20°C

• PFAS-free

• Electrostatically dissipative (ESD)

Selective laser sintering (SLS) material: iglidur® i230

iglidur i230 is characterised as another all-rounder material among the iglidur laser sintering materials. In contrast to iglidur i3, however, it is designed for higher application temperatures and can withstand long-term temperatures of up to 110°C. The material is also 80% more wear-resistant than PA12. Of all iglidur laser sintering materials, iglidur i230 has the highest flexural strength and is also electrostatically dissipative.

When do I use it?

• When a PFAS-free material is required

• If the application temperature is above 80°C for a long time and at a maximum of 110°C

• If components with high mechanical strength (including structural components) are required

• If components with ESD specifications are required up to 110°C application temperature

When should I not use it?

• If components with higher wear resistance at max. 80°C application temperature are required (→ iglidur i3)

• If components with ESD specification up to 80°C application temperature are required (→ iglidur i8-ESD or iglidur i9-ESD)

• For worm wheel applications (→ iglidur i6)

• If the application requires a food-safe material (→ iglidur i6)

• If components with higher flexibility are required (→ iglidur i10)

• If the long-term application temperature is higher than 110°C (→ iglidur A350 or iglidur RW370)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.I3-PL:
• High wear resistance and maximum strength
• Good mechanical properties
• Accurate in detail with precise surfaces
• For sliding applications and gears
• Predictable service life of gears

Selective laser sintering (SLS) material: iglidur® i3

In tribological tests in the igus® test laboratory, iglidur® i3 showed 3 to 30 times better abrasion resistance than conventional materials for laser sintering. This further increases the degree of freedom when designing sliding components subject to wear. It is used for all sliding applications, as well as rollers, gear racks, spur gears and bevel gears.

When do I use it?
• For all types of applications where low friction and high wear resistance are important
• When a material with high strength is required
• When components with high mechanical strength are required, in particular spur gears, bevel gears, drive nuts, special sliders and spherical bearings
• Particularly good wear properties with anodised aluminium as a mating partner

When not to use it?
• For use under water or with high humidity (→ iglidur i8-ESD)
• If components with ESD specifications are required (→ iglidur i8-ESD or iglidur i9-ESD)
• For worm wheel applications (→ iglidur i6)
• When a food-safe material is required (→ iglidur i6)
• When components with higher flexibility are required (→ iglidur i10)
• If the long-term application temperature is higher than 80°C (→ iglidur i230 or iglidur A350 or iglidur RW370)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.I3000-PR:
• Extremely wear-resistant: min. 30 to 60 times longer service life compared to conventional 3D printing resins

• Enormous strength and stiffness (flexural strength 90MPa)

• Self-lubricating and low-maintenance

• High level of detail: resolution of up to 35μm

• Gears from the finest tooth modulus of 0.2

First 3D printing resin for wear-resistant parts and gears: iglidur i3000

iglidur i3000 is suitable for processing on DLP and LCD printers and was specially developed for printing gears and wear-resistant parts. With a service life at least 30 to 60 times longer than conventional 3D printing resins, iglidur i3000 is suitable for all types of wear applications. Thanks to the high resolution of up to 35μm, very filigree components with the finest details can be printed and, thanks to the enrichment with solid lubricants, the components are also self-lubricating and low-maintenance. The mechanical specifications are largely isotropic thanks to the photopolymerisation process.

In its in-house 3D printing service, igus processes its own resin iglidur i3000 using the classic DLP process with a high resolution that enables detailed moulds and cut-outs. In addition, the UV wave length of 385nm reduces excessive hardening and ensures a more precise mould.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.I6-PL:
• Food compliant according to FDA
• High abrasion resistance
• High toughness
• Accurate to detail with precise surfaces
• Ideal for worm gears


Selective laser sintering (SLS) material: iglidur® i6

iglidur i6 was specially developed for use as a worm wheel and for food contact. The tests in the igus test laboratory resulted in a longer service life than for conventionally (e.g. POM) milled gears. This significantly increases flexibility when designing components subject to wear. Furthermore, iglidur i6 is very suitable for snap-fit connections due to its higher elongation at break.

When do I use it?
• When a food-compliant material is required
• For applications with sliding motion, especially for worm wheels
• If a white base material is required for subsequent colouring

When do I not use it?
• If a visually detectable material in blue colour is required (→ iglidur i6-BLUE)
• For applications under water or with high humidity (→ iglidur i8-ESD)
• If components with ESD specification are required (→ iglidur i8-ESD or iglidur i9-ESD)
• In combination with anodised aluminium as a mating partner (→ iglidur i3)
• For gear applications (→ iglidur i3)
• If the long-term application temperature is higher than 80°C (→ iglidur A350 or iglidur RW370)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.I6-BLUE-PL:
• Food compliant according to FDA

• Blue colour for optical detectability

• High abrasion resistance

• High toughness

• Accurate to detail with precise surfaces

• Ideal for worm gears

Selective laser sintering (SLS) material: iglidur® i6-BLUE

iglidur i6-BLUE was specially developed for use as a worm wheel and for food contact. The tests in the igus test laboratory resulted in a higher service life than for conventionally (e.g. POM) milled gears. This significantly increases flexibility when designing components subject to wear. Furthermore, iglidur i6-BLUE is very suitable for snap-fit connections due to its higher elongation at break.

When do I use it?

• When a food-compliant material in a solid blue colour is required (optical detectability)

• For applications with sliding movement, especially for worm wheels

When do I not use it?

• For applications under water or with high humidity (→ iglidur i8-ESD)

• If components with ESD specification are required (→ iglidur i8-ESD or iglidur i9-ESD)

• In combination with anodised aluminium as a mating partner (→ iglidur i3)

• For gear wheel applications (→ iglidur i3)

• If the long-term application temperature is higher than 80°C (→ iglidur A350 or iglidur RW370)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.I8-ESD:
• Electrostatically dissipative (ESD)

• High abrasion resistance

• Increased stiffness

• Colour: black (solid)

Selective laser sintering (SLS) material: iglidur® i8-ESD

iglidur i8-ESD has a high abrasion resistance and is electrostatically dissipative (ESD). ESD specifications are essential in the electronics industry in particular to prevent damage to electronic components. This material is also interesting for all other industries where electrostatic charges need to be avoided. 

• Contact resistance 10^4 to 10^7 Ohm x cm

• Excellent service life - e.g. swivelling: Abrasion resistance factor 2 according to PA12

• Stiffness 60% higher than with iglidur i3

When do I use it?

• When ESD specifications are required

• For applications under water or with high humidity

• When components with high rigidity are required

• When components with high UV resistance are required

• When black components are required

When do I not use it?

• For worm wheel applications (→ iglidur i6)

• If the application requires a food-compliant material (→ iglidur i6 or iglidur i6-BLUE)

• If the long-term application temperature is higher than 80°C (→ iglidur A350 or iglidur RW370)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.I9-ESD:
• Electrostatic dissipative (ESD), without interference currents in live parts

• Contact resistance 10^6 - 10^9 Ohm x cm

• Increased stiffness

• Colour: black (solid)

Selective laser sintering (SLS) material: iglidur® i9-ESD

iglidur i9-ESD has an electrical resistance of 10^6 to 10^9 ohms. This makes it electrostatically dissipative and at the same time reduces the risk of unwanted discharge currents between sensitive electrical components. This makes it ideal for applications in the electronics and semiconductor industry and especially for electronic connectors.

When do I use it?

• When antistatic components with medium conductivity are required

• For applications under water or with high humidity

• When components with high rigidity are required

• When components with high UV resistance are required

• For gear applications under water

• For gear applications where ESD specifications are required

• When black components are required

When not to use it?

• When I need ESD components with better conductivity (→ iglidur i8-ESD)

• For worm wheel applications (→ iglidur i6)

• If the application requires a food-safe material (→ iglidur i6)

• If the long-term application temperature is higher than 80°C (→ iglidur A350 or iglidur RW370)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.J:
iglidur® J | The versatile endurance runner

iglidur® J is characterised above all by the lowest coefficients of friction in dry operation and its very low stick-slip tendency. With a maximum recommended surface pressure of 35MPa, iglidur® J is not suitable for extreme loads.

Typical application areas

  • Vending machinery
  • Printing industry
  • Beverage industry
  • Aviation
  • Cleanroom

When to use it?

  • At high speeds
  • When highest wear resistance is required at low to medium pressure loads
  • When lowest wear is required with a variety of different shafts
  • When low coefficients of friction in dry operation is required
  • When the material should be vibration-dampening
  • When good chemical resistance is required 
  • Best behaviour with soft shafts
  • Low moisture absorption

When not to use it?

  • If high pressure loads occur
  • If short-term temperatures greater than +120°C occur
  • If a cost-effective material is required for occasional movements
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.J2:
iglidur® J2 | Environmentally friendly, versatile and favourable

iglidur® J2 has good universal media resistance comparable to iglidur® J and similar materials. The mechanical specifications in sporadically moving applications are better, but in comparison there are significant reductions in friction and wear. Like all iglidur® materials, iglidur® J2 is PFOA-free.

Typical application areas

  • Motorbike/scooter
  • Robot industry

When to use it?

  • When low moisture absorption and good media resistance with primarily static load is required
  • When a low-cost material for use in a damp environment with low pv values is required
  • When there is basic lubrication of the bearing point

When do I not use it?

  • If a very wear-resistant material is required for continuous dry operation
  • If low moisture absorption and media resistance play a subordinate role
  • If high temperature and chemical resistance are required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.J200:
iglidur® J200 | Especially for aluminium shafts

iglidur® J200 is the result of the development of particularly low-friction materials. Especially for linear technology applications, friction plays a decisive role. While many materials have excellent coefficients of friction under high loads, this material is characterised by the fact that the coefficients of friction are already very good even at low loads.

Typical application areas

  • Vending machinery

When do I use it?

  • For applications with anodised shafts
  • When low coefficient of friction is required
  • When long service life at low load is required

When do I not use it?

  • If steel shafts are intended
  • If temperatures are permanently higher than +90°C
  • If a low-cost universal bearing is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.J260-PF:
• Very good coefficient of friction at low and medium loads　
• Higher temperature resistance: up to 120°C

Wear-resistant filament with higher temperature resistance - iglidur J260

iglidur J260 is available as a material for injection moulding and 3D printing and exhibits very similar friction and wear specifications in all manufacturing processes. This makes it a reliable material for product development from prototype to series production. It has some of the best friction and wear values among the tribofilaments, as well as an increased long-term temperature resistance of 120°C.

When do I use it?
• If the wear resistance of iglidur i150 or iglidur i180 is not sufficient.
• If a higher application temperature of up to 120°C is required.

When should I not use it?
• If the mechanical specifications are very important in addition to abrasion resistance (→ iglidur i180, iglidur i150)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.J3:
iglidur® J3 | The new endurance runner: Specialist for pivoting and pulsating loads

iglidur® J3 is a material with improved wear resistance at low to medium loads and high speeds. The service life is up to 300% higher than that of iglidur® J, the proven top endurance runner material.

Typical application areas

  • Vending machinery
  • Printing industry
  • Beverage industry
  • Glass industry
  • Aviation

When do I use it?

  • When the wear resistance of iglidur® J rotating or pivoting is to be further optimised
  • When very low coefficient of friction is required in dry operation
  • When high wear resistance at low load is sought
  • When low moisture absorption is required
  • When good media resistance is required

When do I not use it?

  • If a wear-resistant material is required for linear strokes
  • If temperatures permanently higher than +90°C occur
  • If radial surface pressures greater than 45MPa occur
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.J350:
iglidur® J350 | Endurance runner with high dimensional stability under temperature - can be used with many shafts and load collectives

A superior material for rotary applications - and that on many different steel shafts. With iglidur® J350, the service life can often be multiplied at loads between 2MPa and 50MPa. The high temperature resistance ensures a wide range of applications.

Typical application areas

  • Vending machinery
  • Automotive
  • Glass industry
  • Mechanical engineering

When do I use it?

  • When I am looking for a highly wear-resistant material for rotations at medium and high loads
  • When I am looking for a low-cost material for high temperatures
  • When a press fit up to +150°C is required
  • When high wear resistance is required at high loads
  • When the material is exposed to shocks and impacts

When not to use it?

  • At temperatures above +180°C continuously
  • When the lowest friction is required
  • When a cost-effective low-friction material is required
  • At high peripheral speeds
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.J3B: The proven long-life material iglidur® J3 is now available in black as well. The endurance runner has been specially developed for pivoting and pulsating loads and is also resistant to media.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.J4:
iglidur® J4 | Low-cost material

<span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">iglidur® J4 is the low-cost material for long service life at low to medium loads.

<span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">Typical application areas

When do I use it?

  • When I am looking for a lubrication-free and maintenance-free material
  • When I am looking for a low-cost material for high volumes
  • When I need a low coefficient of friction
  • When I need good media resistance
  • When I need low moisture absorption

When do I not use it?

  • For high loads
  • When I have high temperatures in my application
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.JB:
iglidur® JB bar stock I Low coefficients of friction and wear
The iglidur® JB material impresses with its low moisture absorption and good media resistance. It also has low friction and wear values.

<span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">Typical application areas

  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If a plastic with excellent coefficients of wear and friction is required

free-form-hub/materialsDescription.JUV: Today, bearings appear not only in the form of simple bushings but also as special forms for special uses, in some instances of which they are exposed to UV radiation for long periods of time. igus® has therefore developed a new material: iglidur® J UV. The tribological properties such as friction and wear of the new black material are comparable to those of the very wear-resistant standard material iglidur® J. Initially, a standard plain bearing product range with and without flange up to shaft diameters of 20mm will be available. Bearings made of iglidur® J UV are available upon request in all previous standard dimensions of iglidur® J as well as in individually designed special parts.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.K:
iglidur® K | Versatile and cost-effective for medium temperatures

iglidur® K is the cost-effective universal material for medium temperatures and applications under various environmental influences such as moisture and media.

Typical application areas

  • Printing industry
  • Plastics machinery
  • Medical technology
  • Packaging industry

When do I use it?

  • If you are looking for a cost-effective all-rounder material
  • If it is used in a damp environment
  • If good wear resistance up to medium loads is required

When do I not use it?

  • When maximum wear resistance is required
  • When maximum media resistance is required
  • When a high-temperature material is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.K230: Compared to iglidur® M250, the new iglidur® K230 material offers lower moisture absorption and even more flexibility.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.K250: The new iglidur® K250 material offers a lower moisture absorption and even more flexibility compared to the iglidur® M250 clip bearings.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.L250:
iglidur® L250 | For high speeds

iglidur® L250 is a material especially for high speed, fast sliding movements and low coefficient of friction. The material can utilise these advantages especially at low loads. Applications in which these advantages play a role are fans, small motors, fast-running sensors or magnet technology.

Typical application areas

  • Automotive

When do I use it?

  • For high-speed rotation applications
  • When maximum service life is required
  • For low loads
  • When quiet operation is required
  • For very low coefficients of friction

When should I not use it?

  • When high pressure loads occur
  • When temperatures are permanently higher than +90°C
  • When low moisture absorption is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.L350:
iglidur® L350 | High performance at lower costs

  • Extremely wear-resistant
  • Rotating up to 3.5m/s
  • Temperature-resistant up to +210°C permanently
  • Low moisture absorption
  • Good price-performance ratio

iglidur® L350 is extremely durable. Developed for the best friction and wear values from 1.5m/s, the material clearly outperforms conventional materials in fast rotation operation.

Typical application areas

Mechanical engineering

iglidur® L350 high-speed test

When do I use it?

  • For high-speed rotational applications
  • When maximum service life is required 
  • For high pv value at low loads
  • At operating temperatures up to +180°C (permanent, short-term up to max. +210°C)

When should I not use it?

  • When a universal bearing material for high temperatures is required
  • When medium to high pressure loads occur
  • For oscillating applications
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.L500:
iglidur® L500 | For extreme rotational speeds

iglidur® L500 is a material especially for high speed and fast sliding movements at low loads. Due to the low thermal elongation and low moisture absorption, bearings can be manufactured with very low backlash. Applications in which these advantages play a role are fans, small motors, high-speed sensors or magnet technology.

Typical application areas

  • Automotive
  • Mechanical engineering

When should I use it?

  • For high-speed rotation applications
  • When maximum service life is required
  • For high pv value at low loads
  • For operating temperatures up to +250°C (permanent, short-term up to max. +350°C)

When should I not use it?

  • If you are looking for a universal material for high temperatures
  • If medium to high pressure loads occur
  • For oscillating applications
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.M210: iglidur M210 was specifically developed for the application for medium pivoting loads. In addition, care was taken during development to ensure that the material can be used particularly well for injection-moulded thick-walled bearings. Therefore iglidur M210 is well-suited for applications in which thick-walled bearings are used or replaced, which are at the same time robust.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.M250:
iglidur® M250 | The robust all-rounder with outstanding vibration dampening

The self-lubricating material iglidur® M250 is particularly characterised by impact resistance, vibration dampening and wear resistance. It proves itself particularly well under stresses where a vibration-dampening function of the material is required, e.g. in sports equipment and packaging machines. As it is also capable of embedding dirt, it is also suitable for agricultural machinery and gardening equipment.

Typical application areas

  • Agricultural engineering
  • Mechanical engineering
  • Furniture / Industrial design
  • Textile industry

When to use it?

  • When the material is exposed to heavy soiling
  • For excellent vibration dampening
  • At low to medium speeds
  • When mechanical rework is required
  • Insensitive to edge pressure
  • High impact resistance
  • Thick wall thicknesses according to DIN 1850

When not to use it?

  • For applications in wet areas
  • When very high precision is required
  • For very smooth shafts
  • When a cost-effective material with maximum wear resistance is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.M260: iglidur M260 was specially developed for use in harsh environments. In addition, care was taken during development to ensure that the material can be used particularly well for injection-moulded thick-walled bearings. This makes iglidur M260 particularly suitable for applications in which thick-walled bearings are to be used or replaced that are as robust as possible at the same time.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.N54:
iglidur® N54 | The biopolymer based on renewable raw materials

iglidur® N54 is the first iglidur® material that is largely based on biopolymers. In addition to the fact that all iglidur® materials are already lubrication-free, this is a further step towards a positive environmental balance. Good coefficients of friction paired with service life that allow series use in sporadically moving applications give this material a firm place in the iglidur® product range.

Typical application areas

  • Mechanical engineering
  • Furniture / Industrial design

When do I use it?

  • For sporadically moving applications with low to medium loads
  • For virtually static loads
  • When the environmental balance of a product needs to be optimised

When do I not use it?

  • If a universal standard material is required
  • If there is a high frequency of movement and continuous operation
  • If there are elevated temperatures
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.P:
iglidur® P | The cost-effective outdoor all-rounder - No swelling even with high ambient humidity

Due to its thermal stability and low total moisture absorption, iglidur® P is one of the most dimensionally stable all-rounder materials in changing ambient conditions. iglidur® P is recommended for pivoting and rotational movements at medium loads.

Typical application areas

  • Mechanical engineering
  • Furniture / industrial design
  • Solar technology
  • Sport, fitness & rehab
  • Traffic engineering

When do I use it?

  • When low moisture absorption is required
  • When a cost-effective material for high pressure loads is required
  • When higher precision is required in damp and medium temperatures

When do I not use it?

  • When mechanical rework is required
  • When maximum wear resistance is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.P210:
iglidur® P210 | Specialist for pivoting and roller applications and more - good friction and wear values on almost any shaft

With iglidur® P210, the user has a versatile all-rounder material at his disposal, which has an above-average service life, especially in pivoting applications with medium loads of up to 20MPa. The versatile material has already proven itself in numerous customised solutions and as a bar stock material. Clip-on or pre-loaded designs and use in vehicle interiors are possible.

Typical application areas

  • Agricultural engineering
  • Mechanical engineering
  • Furniture / Industrial design
  • Textile industry

When do I use it?

  • When a universal material is sought for use in damp environments
  • When a very wear-resistant material is sought for pivoting applications in the medium load range
  • When edge loads and impacts occur
  • When the surface pressure of iglidur® J is not sufficient

When do I not use it?

  • When a universal material with the greatest possible dimensional diversity is sought
  • When a material for very highly loaded swivel applications is required
  • When temperatures greater than +100°C occur
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.Q:
iglidur® Q | For high loads - wear-resistant on many shafts

iglidur® Q was specially developed for extreme loads. Under high loads, iglidur® Q is one of the iglidur® materials with by far the best wear resistance. From a radial load of 25MPa, even the highly wear-resistant iglidur® W300 is surpassed. Special, extremely finely distributed solid lubricants in the material ensure that maintenance-free dry operation is guaranteed under any load.

Typical application areas

  • Agricultural engineering
  • Construction machinery
  • Traffic engineering

When do I use it?

  • For pivoting applications
  • For very good abrasion resistance, especially under extreme loads
  • For extreme p x v values
  • If the material needs to be insensitive to dirt

When should I not use it?

  • For use under water
  • If temperatures are continuously higher than +135°C
  • If high edge loads or heavy impact loads occur
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.Q2:
iglidur® Q2 | The durable high-load material

iglidur® Q2 stands for high load-bearing capacity and good abrasion resistance under high loads. The price-performance ratio is outstanding. Solid lubricants reduce the coefficient of friction and support the wear resistance, which has been significantly improved compared to other iglidur® materials, especially for highly stressed pivoting applications.

Typical application areas

  • Agricultural engineering
  • Construction machinery
  • Mechanical engineering

When do I use it?

  • When high dynamic loads occur
  • When, in addition to high loads, shocks, impacts and dirt occur
  • For highly stressed pivoting applications

When do I not use it?

  • If only static loads occur
  • If high pv value with high speeds occur
  • If a low-cost all-rounder material is required
  • If soft shafts are used

free-form-hub/materialsDescription.Q3E: In this iglidur® material, the outstanding tribological specifications of iglidur® Q3 are embedded in a high-strength jacket. The result is low wear combined with even higher resistance to mechanical loads.
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.R:
<span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;"> iglidur® R | Low-cost material, low coefficient of friction

<span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">During the development of iglidur® R, the focus was on high performance and a very low price. In particular, low coefficients of friction and wear were to be achieved in dry operation. Components made of iglidur® R are specifically supported by a combination of solid lubricants. The PTFE-free and silicone-free material achieves outstandingly low coefficients of friction in dry operation and runs largely stick-slip-free.

<span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">Typical application areas

  • Motorcycle / scooter
  • Furniture / Industrial design

Sport, fitness & rehab


<span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">When do I use it?

  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If high wear resistance at low load is required
  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If you are looking for a very reasonably priced material
  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If a very low coefficient of friction is required in dry operation
  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If edge loads occur
  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If low moisture absorption is desired
  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If PTFE or silicone are not approved for the application

<span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">When not to use it?

  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family:Lato, "Helvetica Neue",Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If high pressure loads occur
  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family:Lato, "Helvetica Neue",Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If temperatures are continuously higher than +90°C
  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If the best possible wear resistance is required
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.RW370:
• Fire-retardant according to UL94-V0 and DIN EN 45545

• Temperature-resistant up to +170°C

• 91MPa flexural strength

Fire-retardant high-temperature filament, ideal for the railway industry

iglidur RW370 is an abrasion-resistant high-temperature tribofilament which has been specially developed for use in railway vehicles. As such, its fire-retardant specification conforms to UL94-V0 and EN45545. In addition, it has the highest mechanical strength among all tribofilaments and a permanent temperature resistance of 170°C, which is why it is the material primarily recommended for high-temperature applications.

When do I use it?

• When the application requires high temperature resistance

• When the mechanical strength of the other tribofilaments is not sufficient

• When fire-retardant specifications are required

When not to use it?

• When very high wear resistance is required at high temperature loads (→ iglidur A350)
free-form-hub/materialsDescription.T220:
<span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">iglidur® T220 | For the tobacco industry, FDA-compliant
<span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">iglidur® T220 is a special material for use in the tobacco processing industry. It fulfils the requirements of the tobacco industry (engineering database). The material is free of undesirable or prohibited ingredients, as required by well-known manufacturers of tobacco products according to the 2004 trade show status, and contains no carcinogenic additives such as e.g. PTFE.

<span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">Typical application areas

  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">Tobacco processing industry


<span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">When do I use it?

  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue",Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If the material is to be free of ingredients that are undesirable or prohibited in the tobacco industry
  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If FDA conformity is required

<span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">When not to use it?

  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If high surface pressures occur
  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If you are looking for a cost-effective all-round material
  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">When highest wear resistance at low pressure loads is required
  • <span style="color: rgb(103,118,138);background-color: rgb(255,255,255);font-size: 13px;font-family: Lato, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;">If only freedom from PTFE and silicone is required
